name: Shore V2 — Polyglot Rewrite
description: Complete rewrite of Shore from Python into Rust + TypeScript polyglot architecture with formalized wire protocol (SWP)

userStories.title: Create repo structure and Cargo workspace
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the V2 repo scaffolded with the correct directory structure and Cargo workspace so that all subsequent crates have a home.
acceptanceCriteria: Git repo initialized with .gitignore (Rust + Node + IDE files)
Cargo.toml at repo root with [workspace] listing all planned Rust members
Empty crate directories created: shore-protocol/, shore-client/, shore-daemon/, shore-cli/, shore-tui/, shore-matrix/
Each crate has a minimal Cargo.toml and src/lib.rs or src/main.rs
shore-llm/ directory with package.json, tsconfig.json, empty src/index.ts
docs/ directory containing SHORE-V2-ARCHITECTURE.md
examples/ directory with example config.toml and models.toml
cargo build --workspace succeeds with empty crates
cargo build --workspace passes
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes

userStories.title: shore-protocol — SWP message types and shared types
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all SWP protocol message types defined as serde-serializable Rust structs so that every binary in the workspace has a shared, type-safe protocol definition.
acceptanceCriteria: shore-protocol/src/client_msg.rs — Client → Server types: Hello, Message, Regen, Command with all fields from §3.4

shore-protocol/src/server_msg.rs — Server → Client types: Hello, History, Shutdown, Ping, CommandOutput, Error, StreamStart, StreamChunk, StreamEnd, Phase, NewMessage, ToolCall, ToolResult, SendImage, CacheWarning
shore-protocol/src/types.rs — Message struct with msg_id, role, content, images (Vec<ImageRef>), alt_index (Option<u32>), alt_count (Option<u32>), timestamp per §3.5 Message Object
shore-protocol/src/types.rs — ImageRef struct with path (String) and caption (Option<String>)
shore-protocol/src/types.rs — StreamMetadata struct with tokens (input, output, cache_read, cache_write as u32), timing (total_ms, ttft_ms as u32), model (String) per §3.5
shore-protocol/src/types.rs — ConversationInfo, CharacterInfo shared types
shore-protocol/src/error.rs — Error code enum: ProtocolError, InvalidRequest, NotFound, Busy, ProviderError, Timeout, InternalError
StreamChunk has content_type field: "text" (default) or "thinking" per §3.5
Phase event has phase field with values: "thinking", "text_generation", "tool_use" per §3.5
ToolCall.input is a serde_json::Value (JSON object), not a String
SendImage push event with path (String) and caption (Option<String>)
CacheWarning push event with expected_tokens (u32) and message (String)
All types derive Serialize, Deserialize, Debug, Clone
ClientMessage and ServerMessage enums with #[serde(tag = "type")] for JSON-Lines framing
Protocol version constant (SWP_V1 = 1)
Unit tests: round-trip serialize/deserialize for every message type including new types
Unit tests: Message struct serializes with all fields including alt_index/alt_count
Unit tests: StreamMetadata serializes with nested tokens/timing objects
Unit tests: type field is correctly set in serialized JSON for each variant
cargo build --workspace passes
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Phase 1: Foundation. Reference docs/SHORE-V2-ARCHITECTURE.md §3 for protocol spec, especially §3.5 Message Object, Streaming, and Push sections.

userStories.title: shore-client — SWP client library
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reusable SWP client library so that the CLI, TUI, and bridges can connect to the daemon without duplicating connection logic.
acceptanceCriteria: shore-client/src/connection.rs — SWPConnection struct that connects via Unix socket or TCP, sends/receives JSON-Lines messages using shore-protocol types
shore-client/src/discovery.rs — Read $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/shore/instances.json, find socket path for a given config path, fall back to default socket
shore-client/src/stream.rs — StreamHandler that consumes StreamStart/StreamChunk/StreamEnd sequences and calls user-provided callbacks
Handshake implementation: connect → receive server Hello → send client Hello → receive History
send_message(), send_regen(), send_command() convenience methods
Async (tokio) with Send + Sync bounds for use in multithreaded runtimes
Unit tests: mock server that validates handshake sequence
Unit tests: streaming handler correctly assembles chunks into final content
cargo build --workspace passes
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes

userStories.title: Protocol serialization integration tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests that validate SWP JSON matches the documented protocol spec so that non-Rust clients can rely on the docs.
acceptanceCriteria: Test suite in shore-protocol/tests/ with golden-file JSON fixtures for every message type
Each fixture is a hand-written JSON string matching the exact format from §3.4/§3.5 including Message Object, StreamMetadata, SendImage, CacheWarning
Tests deserialize each fixture into the corresponding Rust type and verify all fields (including Message.msg_id, alt_index, alt_count, StreamChunk.content_type, ToolCall.input as JSON object)
Tests serialize each Rust type and verify the output matches the fixture (modulo field ordering)
Tests verify that unknown fields are silently ignored (forward compatibility)
Tests verify that missing optional fields deserialize to None/default
Tests verify that protocol version mismatch produces ProtocolError
cargo build --workspace passes
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes

userStories.title: shore-llm — HTTP server scaffold and health endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the shore-llm TypeScript service running on a Unix socket with a health endpoint so that the daemon can spawn and health-check it.
acceptanceCriteria: shore-llm/src/index.ts — HTTP server listening on a Unix socket path (passed via CLI arg or env var)
shore-llm/src/router.ts — Route dispatch for /v1/health, /v1/generate, /v1/stream, /v1/embed, /v1/image/generate
GET /v1/health returns 200 OK with { "status": "ok" }
Unmatched routes return 404
Invalid JSON body returns 400 with structured error
Structured JSON logging via pino with service: "shore-llm" field
X-Request-ID header propagated to log entries as rid
package.json has build and start scripts

npm test passes

userStories.title: shore-llm — Anthropic provider
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Anthropic provider implemented so that the daemon can make Claude API calls through shore-llm.
acceptanceCriteria: shore-llm/src/providers/anthropic.ts — wraps @anthropic-ai/sdk
Accepts per-request format from §4.4: provider, model, api_key, base_url, messages, system, tools, max_tokens, temperature, top_p, provider_options
Handles provider_options: cache_control_depth, thinking, budget_tokens
/v1/generate returns normalized response with content, content_blocks, finish_reason, usage (including cache_read_tokens, cache_creation_tokens), timing, model, provider
/v1/stream returns newline-delimited JSON: start, text, thinking, tool_use, done events as specified in §4.4
timing includes total_ms and time_to_first_token_ms
Unit tests with mocked SDK client: verify request translation, response normalization, streaming event sequence
npm run build passes
npm test passes

userStories.title: shore-daemon — Server (accept, route, broadcast)

Tracks connected clients with their client_type and capabilities

Cleans up registry entry on shutdown

userStories.title: shore-daemon — Process supervision
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the daemon to spawn and supervise shore-llm (and optionally bridges) so that shore-daemon is the only command needed to start everything.
acceptanceCriteria: Reads [services] config section
Spawns each enabled service as a child process
Health-checks each service by polling its socket/health endpoint (1s interval, 30s timeout)
Marks service as ready when health check passes
Monitors child processes — on unexpected exit: log, wait 1s, restart with exponential backoff (cap 30s, max 5 retries)
After 5 failed restarts, marks service as failed and logs error
Daemon does NOT accept SWP connections until shore-llm is ready
On daemon shutdown: SIGTERM to all children, wait 10s, SIGKILL
Collects stderr from children and interleaves with daemon's own structured logs
Unit tests: mock child process lifecycle (spawn, health, crash, restart)
cargo build --workspace passes
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Phase 2: Daemon Core. See §5 of architecture doc.

shore-daemon/src/engine/conversations.rs — Conversation lifecycle: new, switch, list, private flag, manifest persistence
Messages persisted to $XDG_DATA_HOME/shore/{character}/conversations/{conv_id}.jsonl
Conversation manifest at $XDG_DATA_HOME/shore/{character}/conversations/manifest.json with private boolean field
Pre-tool text accumulated and prepended to final response
State changes trigger History push to all connected clients
Unit tests: message CRUD operations

userStories.title: shore-daemon — LLM client and streaming consumer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a thin HTTP client that calls shore-llm for completions so that the daemon can generate AI responses.
acceptanceCriteria: shore-daemon/src/llm_client/mod.rs — HTTP client connecting to shore-llm's Unix socket
shore-daemon/src/llm_client/types.rs — Request/response types matching §4.4 format
shore-daemon/src/llm_client/stream.rs — Consumes newline-delimited JSON stream from /v1/stream, relays StreamChunk events (with content_type) to SWP clients via the server
shore-daemon/src/llm_client/stream.rs — Populates StreamMetadata on stream end: tokens (input, output, cache_read, cache_write), timing (total_ms, ttft_ms), model
shore-daemon/src/llm_client/retry.rs — Application-level retry: refusal detection, model fallback

Cache invalidation detection per §13.3: after each response, if cache_read_tokens == 0 and conversation has >1 turn and not first message after compaction/restart, push CacheWarning event to connected clients and log as ERROR
Cache invalidation check respects [advanced] cache_invalidation_warnings config (default true)
Resolves model profile from models.toml config: provider, model name, API key, base_url, provider_options
Passes fully resolved config per-request (shore-llm is zero-config)
Propagates rid via X-Request-ID header for structured log tracing
Reports timing data from shore-llm responses
Unit tests: mock HTTP server, verify request format, streaming consumption
Unit tests: cache invalidation detection triggers CacheWarning when cache_read_tokens == 0 unexpectedly
Unit tests: cache invalidation check skips warning on first message after restart

userStories.title: shore-daemon — Tool use loop and basic tools
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the tool use agentic loop so that the model can call tools and receive results across multiple LLM rounds.
acceptanceCriteria: shore-daemon/src/engine/tools.rs — Tool loop: call LLM → if finish_reason == tool_use, execute tool → append result → call LLM again → repeat until end_turn or max_tokens
Push ToolCall and ToolResult events to SWP clients during execution
Tool dispatch table mapping tool names to handler functions
Basic tools implemented: check_time (returns current datetime), roll_dice (parses dice notation like 2d6+3)
Tool definitions (JSON schema) generated for inclusion in LLM requests
Private conversation awareness: memory tools excluded from tool list when conversation is private
Max tool iterations guard (prevent infinite loops)
Unit tests: mock LLM returns tool_use → tool executes → mock LLM returns end_turn
Unit tests: tool dispatch resolves correct handler
Unit tests: dice notation parsing

Verify messages persisted to JSONL file on disk with msg_id fields

Changelog: append operation records, query recent changes
Flags: create, resolve, query unresolved by type
Database path: $XDG_DATA_HOME/shore/{character}/memory/memory.db
Reads V1 SQLite databases without migration (schema compatible)

userStories.title: RAG pipeline

Returns ranked list of entries with combined scores, capped at configurable limit

userStories.title: Compaction
userStories.description: As a developer, I want compaction converting conversations into memory entries so that long conversations are condensed without losing context.
acceptanceCriteria: shore-daemon/src/memory/compaction.rs — CompactionManager
Uses compact.md prompt template to have the LLM summarize conversation segments into memory entries
Proactive idle timer: background task fires at idle_trigger_minutes after last activity (not reactive on next user message)
Timer resets on every new message, starts on server boot
Message count trigger: compact when message count exceeds threshold
Skips private conversations entirely (both auto and manual)
Creates new conversation after compaction (archives current)
New entries indexed to vector store automatically
Changelog entries recorded for compaction operations
compact command supports --dry-run flag
Unit tests: mock LLM compaction response, verify entries created
Unit tests: private conversation skips compaction
Unit tests: idle timer scheduling logic

userStories.title: Collation (4-phase pipeline)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the 4-phase collation pipeline deduplicating and consolidating memory entries over time.
acceptanceCriteria: shore-daemon/src/memory/collation.rs — CollationManager with 4 phases
Phase 1 — Tidy: split overly broad entries into focused ones (via tidy.md template)
Phase 2 — Collate: merge semantically similar entries (via collate.md template)
Phase 3 — Normalize entities: deduplicate entity names (via normalize_entities.md template)
Phase 4 — Confidence decay: reduce confidence on stale, unreinforced entries
collation_skip table used to avoid reprocessing unchanged entries
Uses cheap tool_model for LLM phases, falls back to primary
Changelog entries recorded for all collation operations
Runs automatically after compaction
Unit tests: each phase in isolation with mock LLM responses
Unit tests: skip optimization prevents reprocessing

cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes

userStories.title: Activity tracker and adaptive timing
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the activity tracker computing engagement metrics and the adaptive timing algorithms so that heartbeat scheduling has accurate data.
acceptanceCriteria: shore-daemon/src/autonomy/activity.rs — ActivityTracker
Records per-message timestamps: monotonic clock, wall clock, weekday

Computes: engagement score (0.6 × consistency + 0.4 × tempo_score), weekday-aware hour histogram, peak/trough hours (filtered to current weekday, fallback to global with <5 events per WEEKDAY_HEATMAP_MIN), session tempo, z-score anomaly
Session boundary: SESSION_GAP = 1800s (30 min)
Data sufficiency: SUFFICIENT_DATA = ≥5 msgs AND ≥2 days for adaptive timing; SUFFICIENT_HEATMAP = ≥20 msgs AND ≥7 days for hour-weighted timing
Peak hour: density > avg × 1.5; Trough hour: density < avg × 0.5
Tempo score logistic: 1 / (1 + e^((median_gap - 900) / 400))
Stats cache: recompute at most every STATS_CACHE_TTL = 60s
Session medians window: last 30 sessions; session tempo window: last 10 response gaps
shore-daemon/src/autonomy/timing.rs — Adaptive timing algorithms
τ computation: base_τ = 86400 / sessions_per_day, modulated by reciprocation, engagement, heatmap, personality
Per-roll probability: 1 - e^(-check_interval / τ)
Heatmap curve adjustment: low-activity hours contribute more meaningfully than V1
Unit tests: histogram computation with weekday filtering
Unit tests: τ computation with various engagement/personality values
Unit tests: probability roll mechanics
cargo build --workspace passes
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes

userStories.title: Heartbeat scheduler (5-state machine)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the redesigned 5-state heartbeat scheduler so that characters can autonomously reach out with natural timing.
acceptanceCriteria: shore-daemon/src/autonomy/heartbeat.rs — 5-state machine: Session, PostSessionProbe, Deferred, SocialNeed, Dormant
Session → PostSessionProbe: triggers after SESSION_GAP (1800s) idle, measured from max(last_user_ts, last_assistant_ts), SESSION_PROBE_FLOOR (180s) minimum
PostSessionProbe: one-shot probe, character sees idle duration + current time, can choose a time or decline. Response NOT shown to user.
Time extraction from natural language: parse '8:30 PM', 'in 3 hours', 'tomorrow morning', etc.
Deferred: timer waits for chosen time. On fire: character gets follow-up prompt with original reasoning. MAX_DEFERRAL_HOURS cap (24h). If user messages first, deferred state discarded.

SocialNeed: purely probabilistic. τ = base_τ × recip_factor × engage_factor × heatmap_factor × person_factor per §13.1 Constants Reference. Per-roll: p = 1 - e^(-check_interval / τ). Warmup: WARMUP_TAU = 10800s, WARMUP_CHECK_INTERVAL = 1200s with ±60s jitter.
Dormant: unanswered_count >= DORMANT_THRESHOLD (8). No probes until user messages.
Reciprocation factor: 1.0 if reciprocated, 0.4 if unreciprocated
Engagement factor: 1.5 - engagement_score (range 0.5–1.5)
Heatmap factor: peak 0.7, trough 2.0, normal 1.0
Personality factor: 1.5 - personality config value (range 0.5–1.5)
Anomaly context triggered when current_gap z-score >= ANOMALY_Z_SCORE (1.5)
Uses prompt templates: post_session.md, deferred.md, social_need.md
Pushes NewMessage to SWP clients when delivering autonomous messages
toggle_autonomy command pauses/resumes the scheduler
Unit tests: state transitions for each path through the machine
Unit tests: natural language time parsing
Unit tests: dormant threshold behavior
cargo build --workspace passes
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Phase 4: Autonomy. See §13.1 for full state machine spec.

userStories.title: Cache keepalive
userStories.description: As a developer, I want cache keepalive pings so that Anthropic prompt cache stays warm during idle periods.
acceptanceCriteria: shore-daemon/src/autonomy/cache_keepalive.rs — CacheKeepaliveScheduler
Triggers after >10 min idle, only for Anthropic provider, only when cache_ttl configured
Sends minimal API call (max_tokens=1) every N minutes to refresh cache TTL
Stops after max pings or on cache miss (prefix invalidated — triggers CacheWarning push event per §13.3)
Non-user API calls (heartbeat, regen) inform the scheduler that cache was sustained
Respects toggle_autonomy pause state
Unit tests: scheduling logic, stop conditions

cargo test --workspace passes

Subcommands for all 18 commands from §3.7: send, regen, swipe, log, edit, delete, list_characters, switch_character, list_chats, switch_chat, new_chat, status, list_models, switch_model, memory, toggle_private, compact, toggle_autonomy, config
send maps to SWP message type, regen maps to SWP regen type, all others map to command
Streaming output: renders StreamChunk events as they arrive, thinking chunks (content_type == "thinking") shown in dimmed/collapsed style
Instance discovery: auto-finds daemon socket via registry, supports --socket and --config flags
Rich terminal output: colors, markdown rendering
Exit codes: 0 for success, 1 for errors, structured error display
Unit tests: argument parsing for each subcommand

userStories.title: Prompt template upgrade manifest
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the prompt template upgrade manifest so that stock templates auto-update without clobbering user edits.
acceptanceCriteria: prompts.manifest.json written to $XDG_DATA_HOME/shore/ on first run
Manifest tracks SHA-256 hash and updated_at timestamp for each default template
On startup: template doesn't exist → write default, record hash
On startup: template exists, hash matches manifest → overwrite with new default, update hash
On startup: template exists, hash doesn't match → user modified it, leave alone
On startup: template exists, not in manifest → pre-manifest file, treat as user-managed
Per-character prompt overrides never tracked by manifest
Unit tests: all four upgrade scenarios
Unit tests: manifest serialization/deserialization
cargo build --workspace passes
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace passes
